items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This is a confirmed bug in `Azure.Data.Tables` where `GetEntityAsync`/`GetEntityIfExistsAsync` throw `System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream` when the entity exists (HTTP 200 OK). The reporter is on .NET 10 with `Azure.Data.Tables 12.11.0`.

<details>
<summary>🔍 Root Cause Analysis</summary>

The bug lives in the interaction between the **protocol method** path in `TableRestClient.cs` (the custom, non-generated version) and `GetEntityInternalAsync` in `TableClient.cs`.

**Protocol method path** (`sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableRestClient.cs`, line ~187-188):
```csharp
using HttpMessage message = CreateQueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyRequest(...);
return await _pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context).ConfigureAwait(false);
```

When `ProcessMessageAsync` returns, the `using` block disposes the `HttpMessage`. If `HttpMessage.Dispose()` disposes the underlying `Response` (and hence its `ContentStream`), the stream is closed at method return.

**Consumer side** (`sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs`, line ~797):
```csharp
Response response = await _tableOperations.QueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyAsync(...);
// ... 
var dictionary = SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response); // stream already disposed!
```

And in `SerializationHelpers.cs` (line ~14):
```csharp
using var document = JsonDocument.Parse(response.ContentStream); // ← ObjectDisposedException
```

The stack trace confirms the `MemoryStream` path — meaning Azure.Core buffers the response into a `MemoryStream`, but that `MemoryStream` is being disposed before `SerializationHelpers` reads it.

**Note:** The *generated* `TableRestClient.cs` (in `Generated/`) correctly deserializes the stream *inside* the `using HttpMessage` scope. Only the protocol method path (non-generated) is affected.

**Possible .NET 10 angle:** This may be a regression — either in a recent Azure.Core update (where `HttpMessage.Dispose()` behavior changed), or a .NET 10–specific change in how `MemoryStream` disposal is handled. The fix may also have existed on earlier .NET versions where the `MemoryStream` was not disposed.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🧪 Reproduction Steps</summary>

```csharp
var tableClient = new TableClient(connectionString, tableName);
await tableClient.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();
await tableClient.AddEntityAsync(new TableEntity("pk", "rk-1"));

// Throws System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream
var response = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync(TableEntity)("pk", "rk-1");
```

Can also reproduce with Azurite emulator locally. Affected methods:
- `GetEntityAsync(T)`
- `GetEntityIfExistsAsync(T)`
- Any path calling `GetEntityInternalAsync(T)`

</details>

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Debugging Strategy</summary>

1. **Verify `HttpMessage.Dispose()` disposes the `Response`:** Check recent Azure.Core commits to see if `HttpMessage.Dispose()` was changed to call `Response.Dispose()`.
   - Look at `Azure.Core/src/Pipeline/HttpMessage.cs` and check the `Dispose(bool)` override.

2. **Check Azure.Core version shipped with Tables 12.11.0:** Determine if a recent Azure.Core bump introduced changed disposal semantics.

3. **Confirm the fix is to buffer content before disposal:** In `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary`, one workaround is to load content via `BinaryData` instead of streaming from `ContentStream`. Alternatively, fix `GetEntityInternalAsync` to deserialize before returning from the scope.

4. **Potential fix areas:**
   - `TableClient.GetEntityInternalAsync` — call `ResponseToDictionary` before the `Response` leaves the inner scope, or ensure the stream is buffered independently.
   - `TableRestClient.QueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyAsync` (custom) — extract the response content before the `using` block disposes the message.
   - `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` — use `response.Content.ToMemory()` (reads from already-buffered `BinaryData`) instead of `response.ContentStream`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks</summary>

- [ ] Reproduce in a local test environment (Azurite) with .NET 10 + Azure.Data.Tables 12.11.0
- [ ] Verify if the bug reproduces on .NET 6/8 (to identify if this is a .NET 10 regression)
- [ ] Audit `HttpMessage.Dispose()` in the version of `Azure.Core` shipped with Tables 12.11.0
- [ ] Confirm root cause: does `HttpMessage.Dispose()` dispose the `ContentStream`?
- [ ] Implement fix (likely in `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` or `GetEntityInternalAsync`)
- [ ] Add a test covering `GetEntityAsync` / `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` on .NET 10

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Code Pointers</summary>

| File | Location | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableRestClient.cs` | Lines 177–188 | Custom protocol method — `using HttpMessage` disposes before caller deserializes |
| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs` | Lines 733–810 | `GetEntityInternalAsync` — calls `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` after response returned |
| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Extensions/SerializationHelpers.cs` | Line 12–14 | `ResponseToDictionary` — reads `response.ContentStream` |
| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Generated/TableRestClient.cs` | Lines 610–650 | **Generated** path (not affected) — deserializes inside `using HttpMessage` scope |

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [Azure.Data.Tables source – TableClient.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs)
- [Azure.Data.Tables source – SerializationHelpers.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Extensions/SerializationHelpers.cs)
- [Azure.Core HttpMessage (disposal)](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/Pipeline/HttpMessage.cs)

</details>
